title: DNA extraction from feathers
author: George Olah
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bzu2p6ye

steps.instruction: When collecting in the field, avoid touching the tip of the feathers. If wet, dry the feather on paper towel before storing it. Place each feather sample in a separate paper envelope, labelling: individual sample ID, collection date, location, species, collector's name, etc. Store the envelopes with samples in an airtight dry-box with Silica gel crystals at Room temperature .
For each feather sample, prepare and label an Eppendorf Safe-Lock Tubes 1.5 mL PCR clean colorless 500 tubes Eppendorf Catalog #022363212 with the unique sample ID.
For each feather sample, get: a clean A4 paper sheet a pair of Gloves Contributed by users a sterile scalpel blades Contributed by users .
Put on the Gloves Contributed by users , remove the feather sample from the envelope and place it on the clean A4 paper sheet.

Remove a scalpel blades Contributed by users from the sterile packaging. Large feathers: carefully cut out a window around the blood clot from the superior umbilicus of the feather (this can usually be seen just below the vane). See details in: An overlooked DNA source for non-invasive genetic analysis in birds. Journal of Avian Biology. Small feathers: Chop up the entire shaft of small feathers (<20 mm). Dry blood clot from the superior umbilicus of a large macaw feather.
Place the isolated sample into the corresponding Eppendorf tube with the correct sample ID. Dispose the scalpel blades Contributed by users to a yellow sharps container.
Put the remaining part of the feather back to its envelope. Dispose the A4 paper and the gloves.
go to step #4 and repeat the steps for each feather sample, avoiding cross-contamination.
Carefully clean the surface of the feather with 70% Ethanol Contributed by users and a paper towel.
Add 180 µL of Buffer ATL (tissue lysis buffer) Qiagen Catalog #19076 to each tube.
Add 20 µL of Proteinase K, 2mL Qiagen Catalog #19131 20 mg/mL to each tube.
Vortex each tube for 00:00:20 .
Incubate 01:00:00 at 56 °C on a block heater.
Incubate Overnight at 56 °C on a block heater.
Vortex each tube for 00:00:20 .

Unpack appropriate number of spin column + collection tubes (according to the number of samples you are extracting) from QIAgen DNeasy Blood and Tissue Kit, 50 rxn Qiagen Catalog #69504 and label them with the sample IDs accordingly.
Add 200 µL of Buffer AL, Lysis buffer Qiagen Catalog #19076 to each Eppendorf tube.
Vortex each Eppendorf tube with the samples for 00:00:15 . Spin down briefly.
Vortex for 00:00:15 . Spin down briefly.
Incubate 00:45:00 at 70 °C on a block heater.
Add 10 µL, 1M of DTT (1,4-Dithiothreitol) Merck MilliporeSigma (Sigma-Aldrich) Catalog #10708984001 to dissolve Creatine.
Spin down briefly and add 210 µL of 100% Ethanol Contributed by users to each Eppendorf tube.
Vortex and incubate for 00:05:00 at Room temperature .
Spin down briefly and pipette liquid from the Eppendorf tubes to the correspondingly labelled spin columns. Use 1 ml pipette tips Contributed by users at this step.
Centrifuge 10000 rpm, °C, 1 minute .
Discard collection tube with the filtrate and place the spin column in a clean collection tube.
Add 500 µL of Buffer AW1 Qiagen Catalog #19081 and centrifuge 10000 rpm, °C, 1 minute .
Discard collection tube with the filtrate and place the spin column in a clean collection tube.
Add 500 µL of Buffer AW2 Qiagen Catalog #19072 and centrifuge 12000 rpm, °C, 3 minutes .
Discard collection tube with the filtrate and place spin column into a clean 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube (with lid cut off).
Prepare as many 1.5 mL Eppendorf tubes Contributed by users as samples in your batch, and cut off their lids.
Add 100 µL of preheated Buffer AE Qiagen Catalog #19077 and incubate at 70 °C for 00:05:00 on a block heater.
Centrifuge incubated samples 10000 rpm, °C, 1 minute .

Carefully lift the spin column, pipette up the eluted sample from the Eppendorf tube (~100 uL), place back to the spin column into the Eppendorf tube, and pipette the solution back to the center of the filter. This helps to increase the concentration of DNA in the extraction.
Centrifuge 10000 rpm, °C, 1 minute .
Incubate at 70 °C for 00:01:00 .
Transfer filtrate to labeled Microtubes 1.5ml with screw caps Contributed by users .
Store screw cap tubes in fridge at 4 °C for short-term or in a freezer at -20 °C for long-term storage.
Negative control: label an empty tube as negative control for the subsequent steps.
Preheat appropriate volume of Buffer AE Qiagen Catalog #19077 ( 100 µL x number of samples + a little extra) to 70 °C .
Prepare Microtubes 1.5ml with screw caps Contributed by users and label them for final storage (species, sample ID, date of extraction, amount in μL).
